reasoning: Copernicus data released on Nov 6/7, 2025 reveals that October 2025 was the 3rd warmest October on record. It was 0.16°C cooler than the warmest October (2023) and 0.11°C cooler than October 2024. The GISTEMP anomaly for October 2023 was exceptionally high (well over 1.30°C). Subtracting ~0.16°C from the 2023 GISTEMP anomaly leaves an expected October 2025 GISTEMP anomaly of roughly 1.18°C. This is comfortably above the 1.10°C threshold. Daily ERA5 proxy data tracked throughout October 2025 already anchored the market to a very low probability (0.02) on October 30. Given the official Copernicus report confirms the temperature remained extremely high and nowhere near a drop that would bring it under 1.10°C, the likelihood of resolving YES (< 1.10°C) is minimal.
